title: cepa moly
intro: Cepa moly, also known as Alliaria petiolata, is a biennial plant native to Europe and Asia, commonly found in woodland areas and often mistaken for wild garlic due to its similar scent. The medicinal properties of cepa moly include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of cepa moly include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve respiratory health. Cepa moly can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall well-being and health.
